info.title: API Explorer
info.description: The entire API V2 documentation is interactive and can be tested here. To the right side of every endpoint you will see a box with an example request. You can click on the "Try it" button to send a request to the server right from the docs. You will need to provide an API key by clicking the `ApiKeyAuth_token` blue text.

get.description: Get daily account analytics showing the number of emails sent per day for each email account. Useful for tracking daily sending activity across your accounts.

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Start date for the analytics period (optional). If not provided, returns data for the last 30 days.

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: End date for the analytics period (optional). If not provided, defaults to current date.

parameters.name: emails
parameters.description: Filter by specific email accounts (optional). If not provided, returns data for all accounts in your workspace.

date.description: The date of the analytics entry, in YYYY-MM-DD format

email_account.description: The email account that sent the emails

sent.description: The total number of campaign emails sent on this date by this account, including emails for subsequences

bounced.description: The number of emails that bounced on this date for this account for campaigns - including subsequences

contacted.description: The total number of unique contacts who received an email on this date from this account

new_leads_contacted.description: The total number of new leads contacted on this date from this account

opened.description: The total number of opened emails on this date for this account

unique_opened.description: The total number of unique opened emails on this date for this account

replies.description: The total number of replies received on this date for this account

unique_replies.description: The total number of unique replies received on this date for this account

replies_automatic.description: The total number of automatic replies detected on this date for this account

unique_replies_automatic.description: The total number of unique automatic replies detected on this date for this account

clicks.description: The total number of links clicked on this date for this account

unique_clicks.description: The total number of unique links clicked on this date for this account. Unique meaning from unique leads, not unique links

get.description:
"Get analytics overview for one or multiple campaigns. Specify the `id` field to get the analytics overview for a single campaign, or leave it empty to get the analytics overview for all campaigns.

Note regarding the interest status totals (interested, meeting booked, meeting completed, closed): these are calculated based on the first occurrence of each event per contact by default. To change this behavior and calculate the totals based on all occurrences of the events, set the `expand_crm_events` parameter to `true`. Additionally, there is a 10 minute time window after you change a lead status in which the subsequent updates will NOT insert new analytics events to avoid duplicates from rapid status changes and avoid false inflation of the analytics numbers."

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: A campaign ID to get the analytics overview for. Leave this field empty to get the analytics overview for all campaigns

items.description: "A list of campaign IDs to get the analytics overview for. Leave this field empty to get the analytics overview for all campaigns. You can specify multiple IDs by adding the same parameter multiple times, for instance: `/campaigns/analytics/overview?ids=123&ids=456`"

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Start date

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: End date

parameters.name: campaign_status
parameters.description: Filter by campaign status (only the analytics for the campaigns with the specified status will be returned)

parameters.name: expand_crm_events
parameters.description: "When `true`, calculates the total of all the lead interest status update events instead of only the first occurrence for each contact. This will affect the following fields: `total_opportunities`, `total_interested`, `total_meeting_booked`, `total_meeting_completed`, and `total_closed`. Example: if a lead goes from interested to meeting booked to closed, it will count as 3 events (total_interested: 1, total_meeting_booked_1, and total_closed: 1) when this parameter is set to true, and as 1 event (total_interested) when it is set to false (default)."

post.summary: Place a DFY email account order
post.description:
"Place a Done-For-You (DFY) email account order for your workspace.

**Order types**
- `dfy`: Buy new DFY accounts on new custom domains.
- `pre_warmed_up`: Buy available pre-warmed domains returned by `/dfy-email-account-orders/domains/pre-warmed-up-list`.
- `extra_accounts`: Add mailboxes to domains you already ordered. All active accounts on a domain must use the same `email_provider`.

**Provider limits and pricing**
- `1` Google: up to 5 mailboxes per domain; per-mailbox monthly pricing.
- `2` AirMail: up to 5 mailboxes per domain; per-mailbox monthly pricing.
- `3` Microsoft/Outlook: exactly 50 mailboxes per new DFY domain; per-domain monthly pricing; extra-account orders are not supported.

**Before ordering**
- Regular DFY domains must use supported TLDs: .com, .org.
- Check new-domain availability with `/dfy-email-account-orders/domains/check`.
- For pre-warmed orders, choose a domain from `/dfy-email-account-orders/domains/pre-warmed-up-list`; if none are available, use a regular `dfy` order instead.
- The workspace must have an Outreach plan and a default payment method.


Requires one of the following scopes: `dfy_email_account_orders:create`, `dfy_email_account_orders:all`, `all:create`, `all:all`"

last_name.description: Last name associated with the account

x-enumDescriptions.3: Temporarily paused for maintenance; will be automatically resumed shortly

enable_slow_ramp.description: Whether to enable slow ramp up for sending limits

inbox_placement_test_limit.description: The limit for inbox placement tests

organization.description: Organization ID that owns this account

timestamp_last_used.description: Timestamp when the account was last used

warmup_status.description: Current warmup status of the account

provider_code.description: Provider code for the account. Please make sure to specify the right provider code, otherwise your account will not work.

warmup_pool_id.description: ID of the warmup pool this account belongs to

is_managed_account.description: Whether this is a managed account

dfy_password_changed.description: Whether DFY password has been changed

is_ready_made_account.description: Whether this is a pre-warmed account

sending_gap.description: The gap between emails sent from this account in minutes (minimum wait time when used with multiple campaigns)

reply_to.description: Custom reply-to email address for the account

autofix_failed.description: Whether automatic reconnection attempts have failed. null = in progress, true = failed, false = succeeded.

Def0.description: An email account that can be used to send campaigns

Def1.title: Campaign

id.description: Unique identifier for the campaign

name.description: Name of the campaign

type.description: Type of step. This has to be 'email' always - it's the only supported type for now

delay.description: The delay value before sending the NEXT email. The unit is determined by the delay_unit field (defaults to days).

delay_unit.description: The unit of time for the delay value (minutes, hours, or days). Defaults to days for backward compatibility.

pre_delay.description: The delay value before sending the FIRST email in a subsequence. **Only applicable to subsequences** - this field is ignored for regular campaigns. The unit is determined by the pre_delay_unit field (defaults to days).

pre_delay_unit.description: The unit of time for the pre_delay value (minutes, hours, or days). **Only applicable to subsequences** - this field is ignored for regular campaigns. Defaults to days for backward compatibility.

body.example:
Hey {{firstName}},

I hope you are doing well.

v_disabled.description: Whether this variant is disabled. By default, all the variants are enabled. Please set this to true if you want to disable this variant

sequences.description: List of sequences (the actual email copy). Even though this field is an array, only the first element is used, so please provide only one array item, and add the steps to that array

timestamp_created.description: Timestamp when the campaign was created

timestamp_updated.description: Timestamp when the campaign was last updated

email_gap.description: The gap between emails in minutes

random_wait_max.description: The maximum random wait time in minutes

text_only.description: Whether the campaign is text only

first_email_text_only.description: Whether the campaign is send the first email as a text only

email_list.description: List of accounts to use for sending emails

daily_limit.description: The daily limit for sending emails

stop_on_reply.description: Whether to stop the campaign on reply

email_tag_list.description: List of tags to use for sending emails

link_tracking.description: Whether to track links in emails

open_tracking.description: Whether to track opens in emails

stop_on_auto_reply.description: Whether to stop the campaign on auto reply

daily_max_leads.description: The daily maximum new leads to contact

match_lead_esp.description: Whether to match leads by ESP

x-enumDescriptions.1: Campaign is currently not within its sending schedule.
x-enumDescriptions.2: Campaign is currently waiting for a lead to process.
x-enumDescriptions.3: Campaign has reached its daily sending limit.
x-enumDescriptions.4: All sending accounts for this campaign have reached their daily sending limit.
x-enumDescriptions.99: Campaign is currently not sending due to an error. Please contact support for assistance.

stop_for_company.description: Whether to stop the campaign for the entire company(domain) when a lead replies

insert_unsubscribe_header.description: Whether to insert an unsubscribe header in emails

limit_emails_per_company_override.description: Overrides the workspace-wide limit emails per company setting for this campaign.

cc_list.description: List of accounts to CC on emails

bcc_list.description: List of accounts to BCC on emails

ai_sdr_id.description: AI Sales Agent ID that created this campaign

Def1.description: A campaign that can be sent to a list of recipients

Def2.title: Email

id.description: A Unique identifier

timestamp_created.description: Timestamp when the email was added to our database. This is not the timestamp of the email itself, since the email could have been sent at a different time. Please check the `timestamp_email` field for the timestamp of the email.

timestamp_email.description: The timestamp of the email, as provided by the email server. Please note that the timestamp is not always accurate, as it can be manipulated by the sender or the email server.

message_id.description: Unique email ID from the email server

subject.description: Subject line of the email message

from_address_email.description: The sender email address, populated based on the eaccount

to_address_email_list.description: Comma-separated list of recipient email addresses

cc_address_email_list.description: Comma-separated list of CC email addresses

bcc_address_email_list.description: Comma-separated list of BCC email addresses

organization_id.description: The workspace ID

campaign_id.description: The id of the campaign that the email is associated with (it can be null for manually sent emails)

subsequence_id.description: The id of the campaign subsequence that the email is associated with (it can be null for manually sent emails)

list_id.description: The id of the list (if the lead is part of a list)

lead.description: The email address of the lead that the email is associated with

eaccount.description: The email account that was used to send the email. This needs to be validated to make sure it exists in the user workspace

ue_type.description: Email type based on the life cycle of the email

step.description: The campaign step that the email is associated with

is_unread.description: Indicates if the email is unread

is_auto_reply.description: Indicates if the email is an auto-reply. 0 (zero) - is false, and 1 is true

ai_assisted.description: Indicates if AI assistance was used

is_focused.description: Indicates if the email is focused (is in the primary tab in the Unibox)

i_status.description: Indicates the interest status of the email

thread_id.description: Identifier for the email thread. All the emails in the same thread have the same thread ID

content_preview.description: A short preview of the email content (usually the first few lines of the email)
content_preview.example: This is a preview of the email content.

type.description: Attachment MIME type

error.description: Error description when the attachment failed to upload

attachment_json.description: Attachment metadata for the email. This field is null when there are no attachments.

ai_agent_id.description: ID of the AI agent that sent this email (if applicable)

Def2.description: A campaign email, a reply, a manually sent email, or any other email that's visible in the Unibox

status.description: The request status. Do not use this field to determine the verification status. Please use `verification_status` instead to check the verification status

catch_all.description: Whether this is a catch-all email address

x-enumDescriptions.false: The email is not a catch-all

credits.description: The number of verification credits available after the verification

credits_used.description: The number of verification credits used

domain.description: The domain to use for the email accounts.For regular DFY accounts the only allowed domain extensions (tlds) are .com and .org.For regular DFY accounts - you can check the domain availability using the /dfy-email-account-orders/domains/check endpoint before placing an order.For pre-warmed up domains - use the /dfy-email-account-orders/domains endpoint to get a list of available domains.

email_provider.description:
"The mailbox product to order. Defaults to Google when omitted.

Options:
- 1: Google. Up to 5 mailboxes per domain; priced per mailbox monthly.
- 2: AirMail. Up to 5 mailboxes per domain; priced per mailbox monthly.
- 3: Microsoft/Outlook. Exactly 50 mailboxes per new DFY domain; priced per domain monthly."

forwarding_domain.description: An optional domain to forward emails to. This domain must be different from the main domain.

email_address_prefix.description: The prefix for the email address (the part before the @). For instance, if the email address is "john.doe@example.com", then the email_address_prefix is "john.doe".

first_name.description: The first name of the account owner

last_name.description: The last name of the account owner

accounts.description: List of email accounts to create (only if `pre_warmed_up` field is false). For pre-warmed up domains this field will be ignored because the accounts are already created and can't be changed. Please provide minimum 1 and maximum 5 accounts per domain for Google or AirMail, or exactly 50 for Microsoft/Outlook.

items.description: List of domains and accounts to order

order_type.description: The type of order to place. Please check the docs because this endpoint performs different actions based on the order type.

x-enumDescriptions.dfy: Regular DFY accounts - it will place an order to buy new DFY accounts
x-enumDescriptions.pre_warmed_up: Pre-warmed up accounts - it will place an order to buy new pre-warmed up accounts
x-enumDescriptions.extra_accounts: Extra accounts - it will place an order to add extra accounts to already ordered domains

simulation.description: Whether to run a simulation of the order ot not. If set to true, the order will NOT be placed, your card will NOT be charged, and only a price quote will be returned. We will still check the validity of the order and the accounts, and return the results of the validation (if the order_is_valid field is true, then the order would be valid and could be placed).

order_placed.description: Whether the order was placed or not. If true, then the order was placed successfully. If false, then the order was not placed due to an error or simulation mode was enabled.

order_is_valid.description: Whether the order is valid or not. If true, then the order is valid and can be placed. If false, then the order is not valid and cannot be placed. Use this field when you run a simulation to understand whether a real order would be valid.

order_error.description: The error that occurred if the order was not placed. If the order was placed successfully, then this field will be omitted.

x-enumDescriptions.unavailable_domains: Some domains are not available for order - check the `unavailable_domains` field
x-enumDescriptions.blacklist_domains: Some domains are blacklisted - check the `blacklist_domains` field
x-enumDescriptions.invalid_domains: Some domains are invalid - check the `invalid_domains` field
x-enumDescriptions.invalid_forwarding_domains: Some forwarding domains are invalid - check the `invalid_forwarding_domains` field
x-enumDescriptions.invalid_accounts: Some accounts are invalid - check the `invalid_accounts` field the list of invalid accounts
x-enumDescriptions.payment_failed: The payment failed - please make sure you have enough funds in your payment method
x-enumDescriptions.missing_domain_orders: Some domains are missing orders (when you set the `order_type` to `extra_accounts`, all the domains you want to add extra accounts to must be already ordered) - check the `missing_domain_orders` field
x-enumDescriptions.domains_without_accounts: Some domains are missing accounts - check the `domains_without_accounts` field
x-enumDescriptions.provider_mismatch: Some domains received an `email_provider` that does not match the existing accounts on that domain, or the domain is in a mixed provider state — check the `provider_mismatch_domains` field. All accounts for a domain must use the same provider.
x-enumDescriptions.unsupported_provider: Some domains use an existing provider that does not support extra-account orders through this endpoint - check the `unsupported_provider_domains` field.
x-enumDescriptions.provider_unavailable: One or more requested email providers are not available for ordering right now - check the `unavailable_email_providers` field.

unavailable_domains.description: The list of unavailable domains (if any)

unsupported_provider_domains.description: The list of domains that cannot receive extra accounts through this endpoint because their existing provider is not supported for public API extra-account orders.

unavailable_email_providers.description: The requested email providers that are not available for ordering right now.

price_per_domain_per_month.description: "The monthly price charged per domain. Populated only when the order contains Microsoft/Outlook items (domain-level billing: $20/month/domain for a fixed 50-mailbox bundle). Null for Google / AirMail-only orders."

price_per_domain_per_year.description: The price per domain per year

total_domains_price_per_year.description: The total price per domain per year

total_accounts_price_per_month.description: The total monthly price charged for accounts in the order. For Google / AirMail this is `per-account price × mailbox count`. For Microsoft/Outlook it is `$20 × domain count` (domain-level billing, fixed 50-mailbox bundle).

total_price_per_month.description: The total price per month you will have to pay for the order

total_price_per_year.description: The total price per year you will have to pay for the order

total_discount.description: The total discount you will get for the order at the moment. Discounts are applied automatically when we're running promotions.

payment_method_name_on_card.description: The name on the card used for the order
